methodology.baseline_without_network_model: Iteration 1 mapper cost with the initial logical NetworkMemory proxy values before topology-aware feedback.
methodology.energy_note: AccelForge energies come from the 8-chip mapping run and are scaled by 8x to estimate the 64-chip system.
methodology.final_stabilized_mapping: Last feedback-loop iteration after the network proxy values stop changing or the max-iteration limit is reached.
methodology.first_run_with_actual_network_applied: Iteration 1 mapping, with the actual topology-model network energy and latency substituted for the proxy NetworkMemory cost.
methodology.latency_note: Estimated actual total latency is reconstructed per einsum as max(non-network bottleneck latency, assigned network latency), then summed across einsums. The network-only latency is the sum of independently costed collective latencies.

questions: How much is the difference between the energy with and without the network model?
How much is the difference between the first run of the mapper, but including the network energy and latency costs, and the final mapper run?

collectives.reason: Both inputs shard the same contracting dimension, so local partial products must be reduced across chips.
